heading: Expanded access policy required for investigational drugs
label: 360bbb–0 Expanded access policy required for investigational drugs
notes: Amendments2017—Subsec. (f). substituted “earlier” for “later” in introductory provisions, added par. (2), redesignated former par. (2) as (1), and struck out former par. (1) which read as follows: “the date that is 60 calendar days after ; or”.Pub. L. 115–52December 13, 2016
amendments: Amendments2017—Subsec. (f). substituted “earlier” for “later” in introductory provisions, added par. (2), redesignated former par. (2) as (1), and struck out former par. (1) which read as follows: “the date that is 60 calendar days after ; or”.Pub. L. 115–52December 13, 2016

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The manufacturer or distributor of one or more investigational drugs for the diagnosis, monitoring, or treatment of one or more serious diseases or conditions shall make available the policy of the manufacturer or distributor on evaluating and responding to requests submitted under for provision of such a drug.section 360bbb(b) of this title

children.heading: Public availability of expanded access policy
children.label: b Public availability of expanded access policy
children.text: The policies under subsection (a) shall be made public and readily available, such as by posting such policies on a publicly available Internet website. Such policies may be generally applicable to all investigational drugs of such manufacturer or distributor.
contact information for the manufacturer or distributor to facilitate communication about requests described in subsection (a);
the general criteria the manufacturer or distributor will use to evaluate such requests for individual patients, and for responses to such requests;
the length of time the manufacturer or distributor anticipates will be necessary to acknowledge receipt of such requests; and
a hyperlink or other reference to the clinical trial record containing information about the expanded access for such drug that is required under .section 282(j)(2)(A)(ii)(II)(gg) of title 42

children.heading: No guarantee of access
children.label: d No guarantee of access
children.text: The posting of policies by manufacturers and distributors under subsection (a) shall not serve as a guarantee of access to any specific investigational drug by any individual patient.

children.heading: Revised policy
children.label: e Revised policy
children.text: Nothing in this section shall prevent a manufacturer or distributor from revising a policy required under this section at any time.

the first initiation of a phase 2 or phase 3 study (as such terms are defined in section 312.21(b) and (c) of title 21, Code of Federal Regulations (or any successor regulations)) with respect to such investigational drug; or

as applicable, 15 days after the drug receives a designation as a breakthrough therapy, fast track product, or regenerative advanced therapy under subsection (a), (b), or (g), respectively, of .section 356 of this title
